mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the unemployment rate reflects tightening labor markets that boost corporate earnings expectations and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in declining UNRATE periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5%, the cost of capital compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days as discount rates adjust upward and growth expectations fall.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the rate crossing above 4.5%, or the cross-sectional predictive power vanishes after controlling for VIX.

mechanism_narrative: The joint behavior of rising industrial production and falling housing starts signals a rotation from cyclical construction to manufacturing and tech, which historically precedes positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as capital reallocates to higher-margin sectors.
failure_condition: The interaction term (INDPRO up / HOUST down) fails to predict positive 60-day forward returns in > 55% of historical instances, or the combined signal shows no incremental explanatory power over INDPRO alone.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery periods, a sustained rise in total nonfarm payrolls signals robust labor demand that translates directly into higher corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns in identified recovery periods do not exceed the historical median by at least 0.5%, or the signal reverses direction when GDP growth falls below 1.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly contraction in real GDP growth below 1.0% signals an impending recessionary environment that erodes corporate profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in > 50% of quarters where GDP growth falls below 1.0%, or the predictive relationship disappears after controlling for Fed policy shifts.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous occurrence of rising unemployment and a tightening monetary stance amplifies recession risk premiums, compressing equity valuations and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon as market participants price in higher default probabilities.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 5-day forward returns in > 60% of historical instances, or the interaction term shows no statistical significance in a multivariate regression controlling for market volatility.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained uptrend in the industrial production index reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and healthy supply-side fundamentals, which historically supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as earnings visibility improves.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rising INDPRO periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When real savings and deposits decline by more than 5% over a three-month period, it signals household balance sheet stress that reduces consumer spending and corporate revenues, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in > 55% of instances where RSAFS declines exceed 5%, or the signal loses predictive power after adjusting for interest rate changes.
